pg145-p1-s1: and will not be upset by anything he wishes to say, he will probably be off to a fast start.
pg145-p1: and will not be upset by anything he wishes to say, he will probably be off to a fast start.
pg145-p2-s1: In this connection, can you remain undisturbed if the man proceeds to tell you shocking things?
pg145-p2-s2:
He may, for example,
reveal that he has padded his expense account or that he has planned to take your best customers away from you.
pg145-p2-s3:
In fact,
he may say almost anything if he has accepted our solution which, as you know, demands rigorous honesty.
pg145-p2-s4:
Can you charge
this off as you would a bad account and start fresh with him?
pg145-p2-s5: If he owes you money you may wish to make terms.
pg145-p2:
In this connection, can you remain undisturbed if the man proceeds to tell you shocking things? He may, for example,
reveal that he has padded his expense account or that he has planned to take your best customers away from you. In fact,
he may say almost anything if he has accepted our solution which, as you know, demands rigorous honesty. Can you charge
this off as you would a bad account and start fresh with him? If he owes you money you may wish to make terms.
pg145-p3-s1: If he speaks of his home situation, you can undoubtedly make helpful suggestions.
pg145-p3-s2:
Can he talk frankly with you so long
as he does not bear business tales or criticize his associates?
pg145-p3-s3:
With this kind of employee such an attitude will command
undying loyalty.
pg145-p3:
If he speaks of his home situation, you can undoubtedly make helpful suggestions. Can he talk frankly with you so long
as he does not bear business tales or criticize his associates? With this kind of employee such an attitude will command
undying loyalty.
pg145-p4-s1: The greatest enemies of us alcoholics are resentment, jealousy, envy, frustration, and fear.
pg145-p4-s2:
Wherever men are gathered
together in business there will be rivalries and, arising out of these, a certain amount of office politics.
pg145-p4-s3:
Sometimes
we alcoholics have an idea that people are trying to pull us down.
pg145-p4-s4: Often this is not so at all.
pg145-p4-s5:
But sometimes our
drinking will be used politically.
pg145-p4:
The greatest enemies of us alcoholics are resentment, jealousy, envy, frustration, and fear. Wherever men are gathered
together in business there will be rivalries and, arising out of these, a certain amount of office politics. Sometimes
we alcoholics have an idea that people are trying to pull us down. Often this is not so at all. But sometimes our
drinking will be used politically.
pg145-p5-s1:
One instance comes to mind in which a malicious individual was always making friendly little jokes about an alcoholic's
drinking exploits.
pg145-p5-s2: In this way he was slyly carrying tales.
pg145-p5-s3:
In another case, an alcoholic was sent to a hospital for
treatment.
pg145-p5-s4: Only a few knew of it at first but, within a short time, it was billboarded throughout the entire company.
pg145-p5-s5: Naturally this sort of thing decreased the man's chance of recovery.
pg145-p5:
